title: Iceland 4K Drone - Kirkjufell, Waterfalls & Glacier Lagoon Aerial Film
creator: Dronography
notes: DRAFT CANDIDATE -- produced in a cloud (Cowork) session; not yet cross-checked against the live exemplars directory for duplicates at the time of drafting (a freshness check is performed immediately before commit). Kirkjufell is Iceland's single most-photographed/most-droned mountain (the classic Snaefellsnes Peninsula silhouette-and-waterfall composition), so this candidate anchors the location's most recognizable shot within this batch's Iceland-gap-filling slot. This is a REPLACEMENT for an earlier same-slot candidate this session: the first Kirkjufell candidate found (channel 'Quench Your Adventure', watch?v=XY6zUkGs4sE) was oEmbed-verified but, on a follow-up WebFetch of its channel-about page, turned out to be a family travel/lifestyle vlog (creators Jenna & Jeremy; channel description centers on 'family travel tips, pregnancy & postpartum, body empowerment') rather than a dedicated drone-cinematography source -- a materially weaker fit for this reference pack's purpose, so it was dropped in favor of this candidate rather than included at face value. A second earlier candidate (watch?v=6ty71SSnHmw, 'Hindsight Aerial 4k') failed oEmbed verification with an HTTP 401 error and was also not used. Verified this session via WebFetch of the YouTube oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CMYogNF8EXs&format=json), which returned title='Iceland 4K Drone - Kirkjufell, Waterfalls & Glacier Lagoon Aerial Film', author_name='Dronography', author_url='https://www.youtube.com/@Dronography360', provider_name='YouTube' -- confirms a real, currently-public video; the channel name itself ('Dronography', a term-of-art portmanteau) signals a dedicated aerial-videography operation. The watch-page fetch (for CC-license/upload-date/duration/description) returned HTTP 429 (rate-limited) this session, consistent with every other candidate processed this session; not retried further or worked around, so `license_category` is the conservative all-rights-reserved default on absence of a positive CC finding -- re-check when reachable. A WebSearch this session ('"Dronography" youtube channel aerial film subscribers') confirmed the exact channel exists as a distinct, findable result among several similarly-named 'Dronography'-branded channels, but surfaced no subscriber count, press coverage, or analytics-tracker listing -- moderate, not strong, corroboration. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: no contest/broadcaster/festival attribution found. `scores` are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated`, calibrated at approximately the same tier as ecksplorer-faroe-mulafossur-waterfall.json (0.75/0.74/0.77), very slightly below it since eCKsplorer's corroboration included a curated-list feature and dedicated external website whereas this channel's corroboration this session was limited to confirming the channel's existence. `composite_score` is NOT a call to the repo's real `compute_composite_score`/`default_weights()` functions (no shell access to the user's local Python environment this session); it was computed via an equivalent equal-weighted-mean-of-present-signals formula run in this session's own Python: (0.74+0.73+0.76)/3 = 0.7433333333333333, rounded to 0.7433. Re-run through the real repo function before treating this figure as certified. `composition` stays null, matching Milestone 1 convention.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Not independently reviewed via playback this session. 'long-form-cinematic' is inferred from the title convention ('Aerial Film', a landscape-oriented multi-landmark compilation, not a /shorts/ URL) rather than an actual viewing -- correct if a live-playback review finds otherwise.
